claims.statement: This document covers firmware-level audio format support derived from binary analysis of the CPC200-CCPA adapter.
claims.quote: This document covers firmware-level audio format support derived from binary analysis of the CPC200-CCPA adapter.

claims.statement: The document status is 'Documented from binary analysis'.
Source of analysis is ARMadb-driver_unpacked and ARMiPhoneIAP2_unpacked binary analysis.
The binary analysis reference firmware version is 2025.10.15.1127.
The document was last updated 2026-05-21.
The CPC200-CCPA adapter performs active audio processing rather than simple passthrough.
claims.quote: The CPC200-CCPA adapter performs **active audio processing** rather than simple passthrough.

claims.statement: The firmware includes WebRTC audio processing modules (AEC, AGC, NS) and handles multiple audio streams with different sample rates.
claims.quote: The firmware includes WebRTC audio processing modules (AEC, AGC, NS) and handles multiple audio streams with different sample rates.

claims.statement: The adapter uses a decodeType value (4-byte LE integer) in AudioData messages to specify audio format.
claims.quote: The adapter uses a `decodeType` value (4-byte LE integer) in AudioData messages to specify audio format

claims.statement: decodeType 1 = 44100 Hz, 2 channels (stereo), 16 bits; used for media playback when configured for 44.1kHz (mediaSound=0, non-default); not observed on USB.
claims.quote: | 1 | 44100 Hz | 2 (stereo) | 16 | Media playback when configured for 44.1kHz (`mediaSound=0`, non-default) | **[Not observed on USB]** |

claims.statement: decodeType 2 = 44100 Hz, 2 channels (stereo), 16 bits; used for 44.1kHz media (non-default, only when mediaSound=0) OR stop/cleanup commands (dual-purpose); capture-verified.
claims.quote: | 2 | 44100 Hz | 2 (stereo) | 16 | 44.1kHz media (non-default, only when `mediaSound=0`) OR stop/cleanup commands (dual-purpose) | Capture-verified |

claims.statement: decodeType 3 = 8000 Hz, 1 channel (mono), 16 bits; phone call narrow-band, active for AA phone calls (HFP/SCO 8kHz); not used by CarPlay (uses 16kHz); verified AA Mar 2026.
claims.quote: | 3 | 8000 Hz | 1 (mono) | 16 | Phone call narrow-band — active for AA phone calls (HFP/SCO 8kHz). Not used by CarPlay (uses 16kHz). | Verified (AA, Mar 2026) |

claims.statement: decodeType 4 = 48000 Hz, 2 channels (stereo), 16 bits; used for Media HD / Standard CarPlay; capture-verified.
claims.quote: | 4 | 48000 Hz | 2 (stereo) | 16 | Media HD / Standard CarPlay | Capture-verified |

claims.statement: decodeType 5 = 16000 Hz, 1 channel (mono), 16 bits; used for Siri / Phone / Mic input; capture-verified.
claims.quote: | 5 | 16000 Hz | 1 (mono) | 16 | Siri / Phone / Mic input | Capture-verified |

claims.statement: decodeType 6 = 24000 Hz, 1 channel (mono), 16 bits; used for voice recognition; not observed on USB.
claims.quote: | 6 | 24000 Hz | 1 (mono) | 16 | Voice recognition | **[Not observed on USB]** |

claims.statement: decodeType 7 = 16000 Hz, 2 channels (stereo), 16 bits; used for stereo voice; not observed on USB.
claims.quote: | 7 | 16000 Hz | 2 (stereo) | 16 | Stereo voice | **[Not observed on USB]** |

claims.statement: There are two separate audio-format numbering schemes: the USB decodeType integer (1-7) on the host-adapter USB link, and the AirPlay WifiAudioFormats bitmask advertised adapter-to-iPhone during AirPlay SETUP.
claims.quote: There are two separate audio-format numbering schemes in the system

claims.statement: decodeType=5 (USB) and bitmask 0x10 (AirPlay) are unrelated values that both relate to 16 kHz audio but are independent encodings on different links; do not conflate them.
claims.quote: Do **not** conflate `decodeType=5` (USB) with bitmask `0x10` (AirPlay) — they happen to be unrelated values that both relate to 16 kHz audio but are independent encodings on different links.

claims.statement: decodeType=2 indicates semantic context for stop/cleanup operations (seen with MEDIA_STOP, PHONECALL_STOP).
decodeType=4 indicates standard CarPlay audio output (MEDIA_START, NAVI_*, ALERT_*, OUTPUT_*).
claims.quote: **decodeType=4**: Standard CarPlay audio output (MEDIA_START, NAVI_*, ALERT_*, OUTPUT_*)

claims.statement: decodeType=5 indicates mic/input operations (SIRI_*, PHONECALL_START, INPUT_*, INCOMING_CALL_INIT).
In the Phone-to-Adapter-to-Host playback direction, the path is CarPlay/AA (Phone) via iAP2 to Adapter (ARMadb-drv) via USB to Host App (Playback).
Playback media audio is 48kHz stereo (decodeType=4, default) or 44.1kHz stereo (decodeType=1,2, only when mediaSound=0).
claims.quote: Media: 48kHz stereo (decodeType=4, default) or 44.1kHz stereo (decodeType=1,2, only when `mediaSound=0`)

claims.statement: Playback navigation audio is 48kHz stereo (decodeType=4, default) or 44.1kHz stereo (decodeType=2, only when mediaSound=0).
claims.quote: Navigation: 48kHz stereo (decodeType=4, default) or 44.1kHz stereo (decodeType=2, only when `mediaSound=0`)

claims.statement: Playback alerts audio is 48kHz stereo (decodeType=4).
Playback phone call audio is 16kHz mono (decodeType=5) for CarPlay; 8kHz mono (decodeType=3) for Android Auto phone calls (HFP/SCO narrowband).
claims.quote: Phone Call: 16kHz mono (decodeType=5) for CarPlay; 8kHz mono (decodeType=3) for Android Auto phone calls (HFP/SCO narrowband).

claims.statement: Playback processing is minimal — primarily pass-through with format signaling.
claims.quote: Processing: Minimal - primarily pass-through with format signaling

claims.statement: Unlike mic input where WebRTC AECM explicitly validates sample rates (only 8kHz/16kHz accepted), playback audio has no strict firmware validation; the adapter passes through whatever format CarPlay/Android Auto sends.
claims.quote: **playback audio has no strict firmware validation**. The adapter passes through whatever format CarPlay/Android Auto sends.

claims.statement: The phone/CarPlay determines the playback format during session setup; the adapter signals the format to the host via decodeType but does not reject or validate incoming audio.
claims.quote: The phone/CarPlay determines the playback format during session setup. The adapter signals the format to the host via `decodeType` but does not reject or validate incoming audio.

claims.statement: The phone-to-adapter media stream is not PCM; over AirPlay/WiFi it is compressed AAC-LC (WifiAudioFormats bitmask 0x800000, the type:102 media stream) for media.
claims.quote: over AirPlay/WiFi it is compressed: **AAC-LC** (`WifiAudioFormats` bitmask `0x800000`, the `type:102` media stream) for media

claims.statement: The phone-to-adapter alert stream uses AAC-ELD (0x2000000) and telephony/speech uses AAC-ELD (0x4000000) over AirPlay.
claims.quote: **AAC-ELD** (`0x2000000` alert / `0x4000000` telephony, speech) for the other streams

claims.statement: The adapter decodes AAC to PCM internally before forwarding over USB, so the USB decodeType is always PCM and the host never sees AAC.
claims.quote: The adapter **decodes AAC→PCM** internally before forwarding over USB. The USB `decodeType` is therefore **always PCM** — the host never sees AAC.

claims.statement: The firmware includes AudioConvertor::ReSample at 0x6410 in libdmsdpaudiohandler.so.
The firmware includes AudioConvertor::SteroToMono for stereo to mono conversion.
The firmware includes AudioConvertor::GetConvertSrcSamples / GetConvertDstSamples for sample count conversion.
The resampling functions allow the adapter to handle format mismatches, though in practice CarPlay/AA audio is passed through directly.
claims.quote: These allow the adapter to handle format mismatches, though in practice CarPlay/AA audio is passed through directly.

claims.statement: In the Host-to-Adapter-to-Phone microphone direction, the path is Host App (Mic) via USB to Adapter (AGC + AEC) via iAP2 to CarPlay/AA (Phone).
Microphone processing applied: AGC (Automatic Gain Control) via WebRtcAgc_Process, AEC (Acoustic Echo Cancellation) via WebRtcAecm_*, and NS (Noise Suppression) via WebRtcNs_*.
claims.quote:
1. AGC (Automatic Gain Control) - WebRtcAgc_Process
2. AEC (Acoustic Echo Cancellation) - WebRtcAecm_*
3. NS (Noise Suppression) - WebRtcNs_*

claims.statement: Expected mic format for Siri is 16kHz mono (decodeType=5).
Expected mic format for phone call is 16kHz mono (decodeType=5) for CarPlay; 8kHz mono (decodeType=3) for Android Auto phone calls (HFP/SCO); host must use decodeType from adapter's INPUT_CONFIG.
claims.quote: Phone Call: 16kHz mono (decodeType=5) for CarPlay; 8kHz mono (decodeType=3) for Android Auto phone calls (HFP/SCO). Host must use decodeType from adapter's INPUT_CONFIG.

claims.statement: Only 8kHz and 16kHz pass WebRTC AECM validation; both are actively used: 16kHz for CarPlay/Siri, 8kHz for AA phone calls.
claims.quote: Only 8kHz and 16kHz pass WebRTC AECM validation. Both are actively used: 16kHz for CarPlay/Siri, 8kHz for AA phone calls

claims.statement: The WebRTC AECM initialization function at 0x2dfa2 explicitly validates the sample rate parameter.
claims.quote: The WebRTC AECM initialization function at `0x2dfa2` explicitly validates the sample rate parameter

claims.statement: At 0x2dfaa, the firmware executes 'cmp.w r1, 0x1f40' comparing with 8000 Hz, and at 0x2dfae 'beq 0x2dfbc' accepts if equal.
claims.quote:
0x2dfaa: cmp.w r1, 0x1f40    ; Compare with 8000 Hz (0x1F40)
0x2dfae: beq 0x2dfbc         ; If equal, ACCEPT

claims.statement: At 0x2dfb0, the firmware executes 'cmp.w r1, 0x3e80' comparing with 16000 Hz, and at 0x2dfb4 'beq 0x2dfbc' accepts if equal.
claims.quote:
0x2dfb0: cmp.w r1, 0x3e80    ; Compare with 16000 Hz (0x3E80)
0x2dfb4: beq 0x2dfbc         ; If equal, ACCEPT

claims.statement: At 0x2dfb6 the firmware executes 'movw r3, 0x2ee4' to set an error code and at 0x2dfba 'b 0x2dfce' to return failure for non-matching rates.
claims.quote:
0x2dfb6: movw r3, 0x2ee4     ; Otherwise, set error code
0x2dfba: b 0x2dfce           ; Return FAILURE

claims.statement: The firmware WebRTC processing accepts ONLY two sample rates: 8000 Hz (0x1F40, narrowband voice) and 16000 Hz (0x3E80, wideband voice).
claims.quote:
**The firmware WebRTC processing accepts ONLY two sample rates:**
- **8000 Hz** (0x1F40) - narrowband voice
- **16000 Hz** (0x3E80) - wideband voice

claims.statement: Using any sample rate other than 8kHz or 16kHz for microphone input will cause WebRTC AECM initialization to fail; this is a HARD REQUIREMENT and the firmware will reject the audio and may cause session termination or silent mic failure.
claims.quote: This is a **HARD REQUIREMENT** - the firmware will reject the audio and may cause session termination or silent mic failure.

claims.statement: The sample rate is not hardcoded; it is configured dynamically at runtime.
claims.quote: The sample rate is **not hardcoded** - it's configured dynamically at runtime

claims.statement: Dynamic sample rate flow: audio command from phone/CarPlay specifies decodeType; adapter stores sample rate in obj.SAMPLE_RATE global at address 0x94930.
claims.quote: Adapter stores sample rate in `obj.SAMPLE_RATE` global (address `0x94930`)

claims.statement: fcn.000165fc sets the SAMPLE_RATE and WEBRTC_DELAYS values.
claims.quote: `fcn.000165fc` sets the SAMPLE_RATE and WEBRTC_DELAYS values

claims.statement: fcn.0001655c initializes WebRTC modules with the configured rate.
claims.quote: `fcn.0001655c` initializes WebRTC modules with the configured rate

claims.statement: Firmware logs 'rate:%d delays:%d' at address 0x6a603.
AGC functions are WebRtcAgc_Create, WebRtcAgc_Process, WebRtcAgc_CalculateGainTable, providing automatic gain control.
AEC functions are WebRtcAecm_ProcessBlock, WebRtcAecm_AlignedFarend, WebRtcAecm_StoreAdaptiveChannelNeon, providing echo cancellation (mobile).
NS uses the WebRTC noise suppression module for noise reduction.
claims.quote: | **NS** | WebRTC noise suppression module | Noise reduction |

claims.statement: Binary analysis at 0x5a642 contains strings: 'Not all PhoneCall data Do AGC: %d!!!', 'Not all Mic data Do AGC: %d!!!', 'Not all mic data will be processed: %d!!!'.
claims.quote:
Not all PhoneCall data Do AGC: %d!!!
Not all Mic data Do AGC: %d!!!
Not all mic data will be processed: %d!!!

claims.statement: AGC is applied to phone call and microphone audio; not all frames are processed (buffer underflow or optimization); frame count indicates how many frames were skipped.
claims.quote:
- AGC is applied to phone call and microphone audio
- Not all frames are processed (buffer underflow or optimization)
- Frame count indicates how many frames were skipped

claims.statement: Binary at 0x5b7cb contains string: 'msSpeakerToMic_PassToAEC_=%d frameCnt_needBufferedNative_=%d, needIdleDealFrameCnt_=%d'.
msSpeakerToMic_PassToAEC_ is the speaker-to-mic delay for AEC alignment.
frameCnt_needBufferedNative_ is the frame buffer count for native processing.
claims.quote: `frameCnt_needBufferedNative_`: Frame buffer count for native processing

claims.statement: needIdleDealFrameCnt_ is the idle frame count for processing.
The adapter has an audio mixer that handles different sample rates, with error message at 0x5b7f1: 'Audio mixer error, Channel:%d, SampleRate:%d !!!'.
claims.quote:
**Error message at `0x5b7f1`:**
```
Audio mixer error, Channel:%d, SampleRate:%d !!!
```

claims.statement: The mixer handles multiple channels, sample rate is a mixer parameter, and mixing can fail with mismatched parameters.
claims.quote:
1. The mixer handles multiple channels
2. Sample rate is a mixer parameter
3. Mixing can fail with mismatched parameters

claims.statement: riddle.conf key 'mediaSound' controls media sample rate: 0=44.1kHz, 1=48kHz.
riddle.conf key 'MediaQuality' is the media audio quality setting: 0=CD (44.1kHz), 1=HD (48kHz).
riddle.conf key 'CallQuality' is the voice call quality (Web UI): 0=Normal, 1=Clear, 2=HD.
riddle.conf key 'VoiceQuality' is the voice call quality (Internal): 0-2, maps from CallQuality.
claims.quote: | `VoiceQuality` | Voice call quality (Internal) | 0-2, maps from CallQuality |

claims.statement: riddle.conf key 'MicType' is the microphone type selection.
claims.quote: | `MicType` | Microphone type selection | See microphone types |

claims.statement: riddle.conf key 'MicMode' is the microphone mode (values unknown).
riddle.conf key 'NaviAudio' is the navigation audio settings (values unknown).
riddle.conf key 'SAMPLE_RATE' is the runtime sample rate (not configurable), set by CarPlay negotiation.
claims.quote: | `SAMPLE_RATE` | Runtime sample rate (not configurable) | Set by CarPlay negotiation |

claims.statement: riddle.conf key 'AudioMultiBusMode' is the multi-bus audio mode (values unknown).
The CallQuality setting from the Web UI does not work due to a firmware bug (verified Jan 2026).
claims.quote: The `CallQuality` setting from the Web UI **does not work** due to a firmware bug.

claims.statement: TTY logs show CallQuality bug evidence: '[D] CMD_BOX_INFO: {...,"callQuality":1,...}' followed by '[E] apk callQuality value transf box value error , please check!'.
claims.quote:
[D] CMD_BOX_INFO: {...,"callQuality":1,...}
[E] apk callQuality value transf box value error , please check!

claims.statement: The firmware's ConfigFileUtils fails to translate the host app's callQuality value to the internal VoiceQuality config; this error occurs in firmware 2025.10.XX regardless of the CallQuality value (0, 1, or 2).
claims.quote: The firmware's `ConfigFileUtils` fails to translate the host app's `callQuality` value to the internal `VoiceQuality` config. This error occurs in firmware 2025.10.XX regardless of the CallQuality value (0, 1, or 2).

claims.statement: Even if VoiceQuality controlled sample rate selection (8kHz vs 16kHz), the setting is never applied; all telephony audio uses 16kHz because the CallQuality-to-VoiceQuality translation fails and CarPlay independently negotiates 16kHz (wideband) with modern iPhones.
claims.quote: CarPlay independently negotiates 16kHz (wideband) with modern iPhones

claims.statement: No known workaround for the CallQuality bug; the sample rate is determined by CarPlay's audioFormat during stream setup, not by adapter configuration.
claims.quote: **Workaround:** None known. The sample rate is determined by CarPlay's `audioFormat` during stream setup, not by adapter configuration.

claims.statement: Microphone type command ID 7 = UseCarMic (route audio from car's microphone).
claims.quote: | 7 | UseCarMic | Route audio from car's microphone |

claims.statement: Microphone type command ID 8 = UseBoxMic (use adapter's built-in microphone).
Microphone type command ID 15 = UseBoxI2SMic (use adapter's I2S microphone).
Microphone type command ID 21 = UsePhoneMic (use phone's microphone).
Microphone type command ID 22 = UseBluetoothAudio (route via Bluetooth audio).
Microphone type command ID 23 = UseBoxTransAudio (use adapter transmitter).
Update Mar 2026: Both 8kHz and 16kHz are actively used; Android Auto phone calls use 8kHz (decodeType=3) via HFP/SCO, CarPlay uses 16kHz exclusively.
claims.quote: **Update (Mar 2026):** Both 8kHz and 16kHz are actively used. Android Auto phone calls use 8kHz (decodeType=3) via HFP/SCO. CarPlay uses 16kHz exclusively.

claims.statement: The firmware WebRTC binary accepts both 8kHz and 16kHz.
claims.quote: **Finding:** The firmware WebRTC binary accepts both 8kHz and 16kHz.

claims.statement: Binary evidence at 0x2dfa2: WebRtcAecm_Init code paths accept 8000 Hz OR 16000 Hz; 4 AEC call sites pass 8000, 20 call sites pass 16000; the 8kHz code paths are active for AA phone calls and vestigial for CarPlay only.
claims.quote:
- 4 AEC call sites pass 8000, 20 call sites pass 16000
- The 8kHz code paths are active for AA phone calls; vestigial for CarPlay only

claims.statement: 8kHz is not used for CarPlay because: the CallQuality-to-VoiceQuality translation has a firmware bug; modern iPhones (iOS 16+) always negotiate 16kHz (wideband) for CarPlay telephony; attempting CallQuality=0 produces the error and is never applied.
claims.quote: 2. Modern iPhones (iOS 16+) always negotiate 16kHz (wideband) for CarPlay telephony

claims.statement: Current carlink_native implementation (Mar 2026): on AUDIO_PHONECALL_START, micDecodeType = lastIncomingDecodeType (dynamic: 5 for CarPlay, 3 for AA), then startMicrophoneCapture(decodeType = micDecodeType, audioType = 3).
claims.quote:
val micDecodeType = lastIncomingDecodeType  // Dynamic: 5 for CarPlay, 3 for AA
    startMicrophoneCapture(decodeType = micDecodeType, audioType = 3)

claims.statement: Host apps should use the decodeType from the adapter's INPUT_CONFIG command to set mic sample rate.
claims.quote: Host apps should use the `decodeType` from the adapter's `INPUT_CONFIG` command to set mic sample rate

claims.statement: CarPlay phone calls use 16kHz mono (decodeType=5); Android Auto phone calls use 8kHz mono (decodeType=3).
claims.quote: CarPlay phone calls use 16kHz mono (decodeType=5); Android Auto phone calls use 8kHz mono (decodeType=3)

claims.statement: The adapter sends format information in AudioData command messages with layout: AudioData (13 bytes when command present): [decodeType:4][volume:4][audioType:4][command:1].
Host applications must parse the decodeType from the adapter's AudioData message, use that format for microphone capture (not hardcode it), and send microphone audio in the requested format.
claims.quote:
1. Parse the `decodeType` from the adapter's AudioData message
2. Use that format for microphone capture (not hardcode it)
3. Send microphone audio in the requested format

claims.statement: Binary contains strings 'wifiFormat', 'btFormat', and 'wifiFormat is same as BTFormat %s!!!!!!'.
claims.quote:
wifiFormat
btFormat
wifiFormat is same as BTFormat %s!!!!!!

claims.statement: For WiFi (CarPlay wireless / AirPlay), the adapter tracks the full WifiAudioFormats capability set advertised during AirPlay SETUP: PCM 16 kHz mono (0x10), PCM 48 kHz stereo (0x8000/0x8010), AAC-LC 48 kHz stereo media (0x800000), AAC-ELD 48 kHz stereo alert/telephony/speech (0x2000000/0x4000000).
claims.quote:
**WiFi (CarPlay wireless / AirPlay)**: the full `WifiAudioFormats` capability set advertised
  during AirPlay SETUP — PCM 16 kHz mono (`0x10`), PCM 48 kHz stereo (`0x8000`/`0x8010`),
  AAC-LC 48 kHz stereo media (`0x800000`), AAC-ELD 48 kHz stereo alert/telephony/speech
  (`0x2000000`/`0x4000000`)

claims.statement: For Bluetooth (HFP/SCO), the format is 8 kHz or 16 kHz narrowband PCM only — the BT-HFP voice path used for Android Auto phone calls (and CarPlay calls when UseBTPhone is set).
claims.quote:
**Bluetooth (HFP/SCO)**: 8 kHz or 16 kHz narrowband PCM only — the BT-HFP voice path used
  for Android Auto phone calls (and CarPlay calls when `UseBTPhone` is set).

claims.statement: The 'wifiFormat is same as BTFormat' log line is a diagnostic emitted when the two negotiated format sets coincide; normally the WiFi/AirPlay set is a strict superset.
claims.quote:
is a diagnostic emitted when the two negotiated
format sets coincide; normally the WiFi/AirPlay set is a strict superset.

claims.statement: During AirPlay SETUP (RTSP on TCP 5000, after the BT-to-WiFi handover) the AppleCarPlay daemon advertises a WifiAudioFormats array to the iPhone; capture-verified 2026-05-21.
claims.quote:
During AirPlay SETUP (RTSP on TCP **5000**, after the
> BT→WiFi handover) the `AppleCarPlay` daemon advertises a **`WifiAudioFormats` array** to the
> iPhone.

claims.statement: Each WifiAudioFormats array entry is a struct { audioType, type, audioInputFormats, audioOutputFormats }.
claims.quote: Each array entry is a struct `{ audioType, type, audioInputFormats, audioOutputFormats }`

claims.statement: The audioType field is a semantic role with values: compatibility, alert, default, telephony, speechRecognition, media.
The type field is a stream selector: 100 = main audio, 101 = alt audio, 102 = media audio.
claims.quote: **`type`** — stream selector: **100 = main audio**, **101 = alt audio**, **102 = media audio**.

claims.statement: audioInputFormats / audioOutputFormats are single-bit format bitmasks.
WifiAudioFormats bitmask 16 (0x10) = PCM 16-bit, 16 kHz mono — mic / telephony input.
claims.quote: | 16 | `0x10` | PCM 16-bit, 16 kHz mono — mic / telephony input |

claims.statement: WifiAudioFormats bitmask 32768 (0x8000) = PCM 16-bit, 48 kHz stereo.
WifiAudioFormats bitmask 32784 (0x8010) = 0x10 | 0x8000 combined (16 kHz mono + 48 kHz stereo).
WifiAudioFormats bitmask 8388608 (0x800000) = AAC-LC 48 kHz stereo — media (the type:102 stream).
WifiAudioFormats bitmask 33554432 (0x2000000) = AAC-ELD 48 kHz stereo — alert / alt-audio.
WifiAudioFormats bitmask 67108864 (0x4000000) = AAC-ELD 48 kHz stereo — default / telephony / speechRecognition (duplex).
Captured 8-entry array source is carplay-20260521-101016/ttyLog.txt:570-615.
Captured array entry: audioType=compatibility, type=100, audioInputFormats=0x10, audioOutputFormats=0x8010.
Captured array entry: audioType=compatibility, type=101, no input, audioOutputFormats=0x8000.
Captured array entry: audioType=alert, type=100, no input, audioOutputFormats=0x2000000.
Captured array entry: audioType=default, type=100, audioInputFormats=0x4000000, audioOutputFormats=0x4000000.
Captured array entry: audioType=telephony, type=100, audioInputFormats=0x4000000, audioOutputFormats=0x4000000.
Captured array entry: audioType=speechRecognition, type=100, audioInputFormats=0x4000000, audioOutputFormats=0x4000000.
Captured array entry: audioType=default, type=101, no input, audioOutputFormats=0x2000000.
Captured array entry: audioType=media, type=102, no input, audioOutputFormats=0x800000.
The WifiAudioFormats array is built by AppleCarPlay via _getWifiAudioLatencies / the HUDinfo path; on this unit HUDinfo.plist is empty, so the array shown is the firmware default capability set.
claims.quote:
built by `AppleCarPlay` via `_getWifiAudioLatencies` / the HUDinfo path. On this
unit `HUDinfo.plist` is empty, so the array shown is the **firmware default** capability set.

claims.statement: The mic uplink is advertised via audioInputFormats: 0x4000000 (AAC-ELD duplex) on the default/telephony/speechRecognition rows, plus 0x10 (PCM 16 kHz mono) on compatibility/100.
claims.quote:
The mic uplink is advertised via `audioInputFormats`: `0x4000000` (AAC-ELD duplex) on the
`default`/`telephony`/`speechRecognition` rows, plus `0x10` (PCM 16 kHz mono) on
`compatibility/100`.

claims.statement: The adapter uses D-Bus signals internally to coordinate audio.
claims.quote: The adapter uses D-Bus signals internally to coordinate audio

claims.statement: D-Bus signal kRiddleAudioSignal_MEDIA_START indicates media playback starting.
D-Bus signal kRiddleAudioSignal_MEDIA_STOP indicates media playback stopped.
D-Bus signal kRiddleAudioSignal_ALERT_START indicates alert audio starting.
D-Bus signal kRiddleAudioSignal_ALERT_STOP indicates alert audio stopped.
D-Bus signal kRiddleAudioSignal_PHONECALL_Incoming indicates incoming call notification.
D-Bus signal AudioSignal_OUTPUT_START indicates audio output starting.
D-Bus signal AudioSignal_OUTPUT_STOP indicates audio output stopping.
D-Bus signal AudioSignal_INPUT_CONFIG indicates input audio configuration.
D-Bus signal AudioSignal_SIRI_START indicates Siri activation.
D-Bus signal AudioSignal_SIRI_STOP indicates Siri deactivation.
D-Bus signal AudioSignal_PHONECALL_START indicates phone call starting.
D-Bus signal AudioSignal_PHONECALL_STOP indicates phone call ending.
D-Bus signal AudioSignal_NAVI_START indicates navigation audio starting.
D-Bus signal AudioSignal_NAVI_STOP indicates navigation audio stopping.
The firmware can write debug PCM file /tmp/agc.pcm containing AGC processed audio.
The firmware can write debug PCM file /tmp/mic.pcm containing raw microphone input.
The firmware can write debug PCM file /tmp/echo.pcm containing the echo reference signal.
The firmware can write debug PCM file /tmp/ref.pcm containing reference audio for AEC.
Summary for Phone-to-Host playback: minimal processing / pass-through; audio received from CarPlay/AndroidAuto via iAP2, format signaled to host via decodeType, host must handle the format.
CarPlay and Android Auto both default to 48kHz; the adapter is transparent and passes through whatever the phone negotiates.
claims.quote: CarPlay and Android Auto both default to 48kHz — the adapter is transparent and passes through whatever the phone negotiates.

claims.statement: Summary for Host-to-Phone microphone: YES active processing — WebRTC AGC applied to phone call audio and microphone audio, WebRTC AEC for echo cancellation (supports 8kHz and 16kHz ONLY), noise suppression applied, audio format must be 8000 Hz or 16000 Hz.
Host app should send Siri/Voice Recognition mic audio as 16000 Hz, 1 channel, 16-bit PCM (decodeType=5).
Host app should send CarPlay phone call mic audio as 16000 Hz, 1 channel, 16-bit PCM (decodeType=5).
Host app should send Android Auto phone call mic audio as 8000 Hz, 1 channel, 16-bit PCM (decodeType=3) — HFP/SCO narrowband.
claims.quote: **Phone Calls (Android Auto):** 8000 Hz, 1 channel, 16-bit PCM (decodeType=3) — HFP/SCO narrowband

claims.statement: Firmware-supported mic sample rates (binary verified): 8000 Hz (0x1F40) narrowband and 16000 Hz (0x3E80) wideband; other sample rates cause WebRTC AECM initialization failure.
claims.quote: **Firmware-supported mic sample rates:** 8000 Hz (0x1F40) or 16000 Hz (0x3E80) only

claims.statement: Host app will receive media playback audio as 48000 Hz, 2 channels, 16-bit PCM (decodeType=4); voice/phone format is specified in the AudioData command message.
claims.quote:
**Media:** 48000 Hz, 2 channels, 16-bit PCM (decodeType=4)
- **Voice/Phone:** Format specified in AudioData command message

claims.statement: Binary reference: AudioConvertor::ReSample at address 0x6410 in libdmsdpaudiohandler.so for sample rate conversion.
Binary reference: AECM Init at address 0x2dfa2 is WebRtcAecm_Init which validates 8kHz/16kHz.

Binary reference: sample rate check at 0x2dfb0 is 'cmp.w r1, 0x3e80' (16000 Hz).

Binary reference: WebRTC init at address 0x1655c is fcn.0001655c which initializes WebRTC modules.
Binary reference: rate/delay log at address 0x6a603 is 'rate:%d delays:%d'.

claims.quote: | AGC error | `0x6bff2` | `webrtc_Agc_Process args error!` (distinct from the PhoneCall-AGC string) |

claims.quote: | Mixer error | `0x5b7f1` | `Audio mixer error, Channel:%d, SampleRate:%d !!!` |

This document covers the internal microphone processing pipeline within the CPC200-CCPA adapter firmware (WebRTC, I2S, format conversion).
claims.quote: This document covers the internal microphone processing pipeline within the CPC200-CCPA adapter firmware (WebRTC, I2S, format conversion).

claims.statement: Microphone capture specs apply to both CarPlay and Android Auto.
claims.quote: Microphone capture specs apply to both CarPlay and Android Auto.

claims.statement: The document's overall status is VERIFIED via binary analysis.
The document was last updated 2026-05-21.
CPC200-CCPA firmware processes microphone audio from external sources (host apps) and forwards to CarPlay/Android Auto protocols.
claims.quote: CPC200-CCPA firmware processes microphone audio from external sources (host apps) and forwards to CarPlay/Android Auto protocols.

claims.statement: The A15W model lacks an onboard microphone, implementing bidirectional audio bridging via USB.
claims.quote: The A15W model lacks an onboard microphone, implementing bidirectional audio bridging via USB.

claims.statement: The firmware is an intelligent audio protocol bridge handling bidirectional stream multiplexing, processing microphone data from USB hosts and routing to CarPlay/Android Auto via format conversion and RTP streaming.
claims.quote: Intelligent audio protocol bridge handling bidirectional stream multiplexing - processes microphone data from USB hosts, routes to CarPlay/Android Auto via format conversion and RTP streaming.

claims.statement: A15W variant has no onboard microphone.
The microphone data source is host apps via USB NCM interface.
claims.quote: | Data Source | Host apps via USB NCM interface |

claims.statement: Supported protocols are CarPlay, AndroidAuto, AndroidMirror, iOSMirror, HiCar.
claims.quote: | Protocols | CarPlay, AndroidAuto, AndroidMirror, iOSMirror, HiCar |

claims.statement: The configuration key is MDLINK='CarPlay,AndroidAuto,AndroidMirror,iOSMirror,HiCar'.
The data flow pipeline is: Host App → USB NCM → boxNetworkService → MicAudioProcessor → AudioConvertor → DMSDP RTP → CarPlay/Android Auto.
claims.quote:
Host App → USB NCM → boxNetworkService → MicAudioProcessor →
AudioConvertor → DMSDP RTP → CarPlay/Android Auto

claims.statement: boxNetworkService is the USB NCM interface handler.
MicAudioProcessor exposes PushAudio(), PopAudio(), Reset().
AudioConvertor performs format conversion, stereo→mono, sample rate conversion.
claims.quote: | **AudioConvertor** | Format conversion, stereo→mono, sample rate conversion |

claims.statement: DMSDP RTP handles packet assembly and RTP streaming.
MicAudioProcessor class has methods PushAudio(unsigned char* data, unsigned int size, unsigned int type), PopAudio(unsigned char* data, unsigned int size, unsigned int type), and Reset().
MicAudioProcessor mangled symbols follow pattern _ZN17MicAudioProcessor[9PushAudio|8PopAudio|5Reset]E*.

AudioService class has methods PushMicData(unsigned char* data, unsigned int size, unsigned int type), IsUsePhoneMic(), IsSupportBGRecord(), OpenAudioRecord(const char* profile, int p1, int p2, const DMSDPProfiles*), CloseAudioRecord(const char* profile, int p3).
AudioService mangled symbols follow pattern _ZN12AudioService[11PushMicData|15OpenAudioRecord|16CloseAudioRecord]E*.
IsUsePhoneMic and IsSupportBGRecord are global functions with mangled names _Z[13IsUsePhoneMic|17IsSupportBGRecord]v.

AudioConvertor class has methods SetFormat(AudioPCMFormat src, AudioPCMFormat dst), PushSrcAudio(unsigned char* data, unsigned int size), PopDstAudio(unsigned char* data, unsigned int size), GetConvertRatio(), SteroToMono(short* left, short* right, int samples), GetConvertSrcSamples(int src_rate, int dst_rate, int samples).
AudioConvertor mangled symbols follow pattern _ZN14AudioConvertor[9SetFormat|12PushSrcAudio|11PopDstAudio|15GetConvertRatio|11SteroToMono|20GetConvertSrcSamples]E*.
Processing pipeline step 1 is USB Reception: Host app → USB NCM → boxNetworkService.
claims.quote: | 1 | USB Reception | Host app → USB NCM → boxNetworkService |

claims.statement: Processing pipeline step 2 is Protocol Parsing: extract audio payload, validate packets.
claims.quote: | 2 | Protocol Parsing | Extract audio payload, validate packets |

claims.statement: Processing pipeline step 3 is Processing: MicAudioProcessor::PushAudio() → AudioService::PushMicData().
Processing pipeline step 4 is Conversion: AudioConvertor::SetFormat() → format/rate conversion.
Processing pipeline step 5 is RTP Assembly: DMSDPRtpSendPCMPackFillPayload() → DMSDPPCMPostData().
Processing pipeline step 6 is Transmission: CarPlay (iAP2), Android Auto (AOA), HiCar (custom).
Audio focus is requested via AudioService::requestAudioFocus(AUDIO_TYPE_VOICE_COMMAND, FOCUS_FLAGS).
AudioService has handleAudioType(AUDIO_TYPE_HICAR_SDK&, DMSDPAudioStreamType, bool&) and getAudioTypeByDataAndStream(const char*, DMSDPVirtualStreamData*).
AudioService focus mangled symbols follow pattern _ZN12AudioService[17requestAudioFocus|17abandonAudioFocus|15handleAudioType|27getAudioTypeByDataAndStream]E*.

MicrophoneAudioTypes enum: AUDIO_TYPE_VOICE_COMMAND = 1 (Siri, Google Assistant), AUDIO_TYPE_PHONE_CALL = 2 (Hands-free calling), AUDIO_TYPE_VOICE_MEMO = 3 (Voice recording), AUDIO_TYPE_NAVIGATION = 4 (Navigation voice input).
Config micGain=0 is external mic gain (app-controlled).
Config micType=0 is External/USB microphone.
claims.quote: "micType": 0,           // External/USB microphone (see audio_formats.md for full MicType command IDs)

claims.statement: Config VrPacketLen=200 is voice recognition packet length.
Config VrVolumGain=0 is voice volume gain.
Config backRecording=0 is background recording capability.
Config CallQuality=1 is call quality enhancement.
IsUsePhoneMic() is an external/app microphone check.
IsSupportBGRecord() indicates 'Hey Siri'/'OK Google' support.
AudioService has GetAudioCapability(DMSDPAudioCapabilities**, unsigned int*) with mangled name _ZN12AudioService18GetAudioCapabilityE*.

DMSDP RTP transport functions: DMSDPRtpSendPCMPackFillPayload(rtp_packet_t*, unsigned char*, unsigned int), DMSDPPCMPostData(unsigned char*, unsigned int stream_id, unsigned int timestamp), DMSDPPCMProcessPacket(unsigned char*, unsigned int).
DMSDP RTP has DMSDPDataSessionRtpSender[Callback|EventsHandler](rtp_session_t*, rtp_event[_t*|s_t]) and DMSDPDataSessionInitRtpRecevier(...).

Stream callback functions: DMSDPStreamSetCallback(stream_id_t, stream_callback_t), DMSDPServiceProviderStreamSetCallback(provider_t*, stream_callback_t), DMSDPServiceSessionSetStreamCallback(session_t*, stream_callback_t).
USB Reception performance: <0.5ms, 5KB memory, <1% CPU.
MicAudioProcessor performance: 1-2ms, 20KB memory, 3-5% CPU.
Format Conversion performance: 0.5-1ms, 15KB memory, 2-3% CPU.
RTP Assembly performance: <0.5ms, 10KB memory, 1-2% CPU.
Protocol Transmission performance: 1-2ms, 8KB memory, 2-3% CPU.
Total pipeline performance: 3-6ms, 58KB memory, 9-14% CPU.
Optimizations include 200-byte VR packets, direct sample rate conversion, hardware-accelerated RTP, and USB NCM bulk transfer.
claims.quote:
- 200-byte VR packets
- Direct sample rate conversion
- Hardware-accelerated RTP
- USB NCM bulk transfer

claims.statement: CarPlay protocol integration uses iAP2 audio sessions ('nMic2' config from ARMiPhoneIAP2).
claims.quote: | **CarPlay** | iAP2 audio sessions ("nMic2" config from ARMiPhoneIAP2) |

claims.statement: Android Auto protocol integration uses AOA protocol audio channels via DMSDP transport.
claims.quote: | **Android Auto** | AOA protocol audio channels via DMSDP transport |

claims.statement: HiCar protocol integration uses custom audio processing (ARMHiCar executable).
Background recording uses voice activation 'Hey Siri'/'OK Google' via IsSupportBGRecord(), with continuous monitoring, low-power detection, seamless focus switching.
claims.quote:
- Voice activation: "Hey Siri"/"OK Google" via `IsSupportBGRecord()`
- Continuous monitoring, low-power detection, seamless focus switching

claims.statement: Call quality state management uses AudioService::OnCallStateChangeE(CALL_STATE), with enhanced processing via 'CallQuality': 1.
Voice command assistants: CarPlay uses Siri commands/dictation, Android Auto uses Google Assistant, HiCar uses Huawei voice assistant, Universal supports hands-free calling.
claims.quote:
| CarPlay | Siri commands/dictation |
| Android Auto | Google Assistant |
| HiCar | Huawei voice assistant |
| Universal | Hands-free calling |

claims.statement: Debug functions: AudioService::getCurAudioType(int*, int*), AudioService::OnAudioFocusChange(int), AudioService::OnMediaStatusChange(MEDIA_STATE).
Microphone audio must be 8kHz or 16kHz — the firmware's WebRTC AECM module at address 0x2dfa2 accepts only these two rates and rejects others.
claims.quote: the only rates accepted by the firmware's WebRTC AECM module at `0x2dfa2`

claims.statement: 8kHz was previously thought to be vestigial (based on CarPlay-only captures) but is actively used for AA phone calls.
claims.quote: 8kHz was previously thought to be vestigial (based on CarPlay-only captures)

claims.statement: CarPlay uses 16kHz exclusively.
Host apps must parse decodeType from the adapter's INPUT_CONFIG command and set mic capture rate accordingly.
claims.quote: Host apps must parse `decodeType` from the adapter's `INPUT_CONFIG` command

claims.statement: Android Auto phone call microphone was FIXED on 2026-03-15, confirmed working via Google Meet VoIP call.
claims.quote: **Status:** FIXED — confirmed working via Google Meet VoIP call

claims.statement: The AA phone call mic issue was previously (2026-03-14) believed to be a firmware limitation, but was actually a host-side decodeType mismatch.
claims.quote: **Previous status:** Was believed to be a firmware limitation (2026-03-14), actually a host-side decodeType mismatch

claims.statement: The fix environment was carlink_native on AAOS emulator (sdk_gcar_arm64) + CPC200-CCPA (FW 2025.10.15.1127CAY) + Pixel 10 wireless AA.
claims.quote: **Environment:** carlink_native on AAOS emulator (sdk_gcar_arm64) + CPC200-CCPA (FW 2025.10.15.1127CAY) + Pixel 10 wireless AA

claims.statement: carlink_native hardcoded mic capture at 16kHz (decodeType=5) for all modes.
claims.quote: carlink_native hardcoded mic capture at 16kHz (decodeType=5) for all modes.

claims.statement: During AA phone calls, the adapter switches to HFP/SCO at 8kHz narrowband (decodeType=3).
claims.quote: During AA phone calls, the adapter switches to HFP/SCO at 8kHz narrowband (decodeType=3).

claims.statement: The adapter expected mic data tagged as decodeType=3 for SCO routing; receiving decodeType=5 caused it to route data to the voice assistant path (AudioInputService) instead of the SCO bridge, resulting in zero bytes on the phone call mic path (SndPushInput total 0).
claims.quote: so it routed the data to the voice assistant path (AudioInputService) instead of the SCO bridge — resulting in zero bytes on the phone call mic path (`SndPushInput total 0`).

claims.statement: The initial analysis (2026-03-14) observed SndPushInput total 0 during calls and concluded the USB mic and HFP/SCO paths were unbridged in firmware.
claims.quote: The initial analysis (2026-03-14) observed `SndPushInput total 0` during calls and concluded the USB mic and HFP/SCO paths were unbridged in firmware.

claims.statement: The adapter's SndPushInput path works correctly — it requires mic data at the correct sample rate and decodeType.
claims.quote: the adapter's `SndPushInput` path works correctly — it just requires mic data at the correct sample rate and decodeType

claims.statement: Autokit handles the rate switch via its INPUT_CONFIG handler that dynamically switches AudioRecord between 16kHz and 8kHz.
claims.quote: Autokit (which works) handles this via its `INPUT_CONFIG` handler that dynamically switches AudioRecord between 16kHz and 8kHz.

claims.statement: The fix was applied in CarlinkManager.kt.
Fix step 1: track incoming audio decodeType (lastIncomingDecodeType) from AudioData packets.
claims.quote: 1. Track incoming audio decodeType (`lastIncomingDecodeType`) from AudioData packets

claims.statement: Fix step 2: handle AUDIO_INPUT_CONFIG command — restart mic capture at the adapter's requested format.
claims.quote: 2. Handle `AUDIO_INPUT_CONFIG` command — restart mic capture at the adapter's requested format

claims.statement: Fix step 3: use lastIncomingDecodeType for PHONECALL_START instead of hardcoded 5.
claims.quote: 3. Use `lastIncomingDecodeType` for PHONECALL_START instead of hardcoded 5

claims.statement: Fix step 4: pass decodeType to MicrophoneCaptureManager.start(decodeType) for correct sample rate.
claims.quote: 4. Pass decodeType to `MicrophoneCaptureManager.start(decodeType)` for correct sample rate

claims.statement: Fix step 5: adjust readChunk size to 320 bytes (20ms) at 8kHz, 640 bytes at 16kHz.
claims.quote: 5. Adjust `readChunk` size: 320 bytes (20ms) at 8kHz, 640 bytes at 16kHz

claims.statement: Google Assistant mic uses decodeType=5 at 16kHz; command sequence: AUDIO_INPUT_CONFIG (decodeType=5, 16kHz wideband), AUDIO_SIRI_START (id=8), capture at 16000Hz 1ch, AUDIO_OUTPUT_START (id=1), AudioData(0x07) decodeType=5 audioType=3, AudioInputService open succeed, AUDIO_SIRI_STOP (id=9).
AUDIO_SIRI_START command has id=8.
AUDIO_OUTPUT_START command has id=1.
AUDIO_SIRI_STOP command has id=9.
GA mic capture stopped after 392320 bytes / 12.3 seconds, ~31KB/s which is correct for 16kHz mono.
AudioData packets use type 0x07.

Phone/VoIP call mic uses decodeType=3 at 8kHz; sequence includes AUDIO_INCOMING_CALL_INIT (id=14), AUDIO_ALERT_START (id=12), SndPushOutput 320 samples, AUDIO_ALERT_STOP (id=13), AUDIO_PHONECALL_START (id=4), capture at 8000Hz 1ch, AUDIO_INPUT_CONFIG (decodeType=3), AudioData(0x07) decodeType=3 audioType=3, SndPushInput 320 samples (non-zero), Sco send 240.
AUDIO_INCOMING_CALL_INIT command has id=14.
AUDIO_ALERT_START command has id=12.
AUDIO_ALERT_STOP command has id=13.
AUDIO_PHONECALL_START command has id=4.
During the phone call sequence the ringtone is played via BT SCO (SndPushOutput 320 samples).
claims.quote: SndPushOutput 320 samples                ← ringtone via BT SCO

claims.statement: During the phone call, mic data is forwarded to the phone via BT SCO (Sco send 240).
claims.quote: Sco send 240, total N                    ← mic forwarded to phone via BT SCO

claims.statement: BOX_TMP_DATA_AUDIO_TYPE bitmask 0x0000 means Idle.
BOX_TMP_DATA_AUDIO_TYPE bitmask 0x0001 means Phone call mode.
BOX_TMP_DATA_AUDIO_TYPE bitmask 0x0004 means Siri/GA audio output.
BOX_TMP_DATA_AUDIO_TYPE bitmask 0x0008 means Alert/ringtone.
BOX_TMP_DATA_AUDIO_TYPE bitmask 0x0100 means Mic recording flag.
BOX_TMP_DATA_AUDIO_TYPE bitmask 0x0400 means INPUT_CONFIG received.
BOX_TMP_DATA_AUDIO_TYPE bitmask 0x0504 means Recording + Siri output (GA active).
BOX_TMP_DATA_AUDIO_TYPE bitmask 0x0501 means Recording + phone call (VOIP active).
Adapter VOIP call flow (ttyLog) was confirmed working 2026-03-15, showing bitmask transitions 0x0008 → 0x0108 → 0x0001 → 0x0401 → 0x0501.
claims.quote: ### Adapter VOIP Call Flow (ttyLog, confirmed working 2026-03-15)

claims.statement: During VOIP flow the adapter sends _SendPhoneCommandToCar: StartRecordMic(1) command to host to start mic.
claims.quote: _SendPhoneCommandToCar: StartRecordMic(1)       ← command to host: start mic

claims.statement: SndPushInput first sync caches 60 ms.
claims.quote: SndPushInput first sync, cache 60 ms            ← mic data arriving from host

claims.statement: During VOIP flow SndPushInput showed 320 samples, total 16000 = 16000 samples/sec = 16kHz on ALSA.
claims.quote: SndPushInput 320 samples, total 16000           ← 16000 samples/sec = 16kHz on ALSA

claims.statement: During VOIP flow Sco send showed 240, total 256080 forwarded to phone via BT SCO.
claims.quote: Sco send 240, total 256080                      ← forwarded to phone via BT SCO

claims.statement: During VOIP flow Sco read showed 120, total 480000 — caller voice received (bidirectional).
claims.quote: Sco read 120, total 480000                      ← caller voice received (bidirectional)

claims.statement: decodeType 3 = 8000 Hz, 1 channel, phone call (narrowband), used for AA VOIP/cellular calls.
claims.quote: decodeType=3 (8000 Hz mono — phone call narrowband, AA VOIP/cellular calls)

claims.statement: decodeType 5 = 16000 Hz, 1 channel, voice assistant (wideband), used for GA, Siri, CarPlay calls.
claims.quote: decodeType=5 (16000 Hz mono — voice assistant wideband: GA, Siri, CarPlay calls)

claims.statement: CarPlay phone calls use decodeType=5 (16kHz) because iPhone negotiates wideband telephony over iAP2/WiFi (not HFP/SCO).
claims.quote: CarPlay phone calls also use decodeType=5 (16kHz) because iPhone negotiates wideband telephony over iAP2/WiFi (not HFP/SCO).

claims.statement: During VOIP call requestAudioFocus uses USAGE_VOICE_COMMUNICATION/CONTENT_TYPE_SPEECH (PHONE_CALL focus).
During AA VOIP call AUDIO_PERF shows active:8000Hz 8000Hz:50ms(ACTIVE) Focus:[RINGTONE,PHONE_CALL].
During VOIP call Pixel shows AOC: VoIP session 2: Tx:9600, Rx:9600 (bidirectional audio).

During active VOIP, ARMAndroidAuto is active at PID 1183, 70MB VSZ — OpenAuto AA protocol handler.

During active VOIP, boxNetworkService is active as USB NCM interface for mic/audio data.

claims.statement: SCO MTU is 240:32, Voice setting 0x0060.
Autokit GA mic rate is 16kHz (htlog: rate:16000); carlink_native is 16kHz (Capture started: 16000Hz).
Autokit call mic rate is 8kHz (htlog: rate:8000); carlink_native is 8kHz (Capture started: 8000Hz).
Autokit INPUT_CONFIG handling: a.i = w.a; h.h(c(w.a, true)) — restart AudioRecord; carlink_native: stopMicrophoneCapture(); startMicrophoneCapture(decodeType).
claims.quote: | INPUT_CONFIG handling | `a.i = w.a; h.h(c(w.a, true))` — restart AudioRecord | `stopMicrophoneCapture(); startMicrophoneCapture(decodeType)` |

Both Autokit and carlink_native send mic packet volume 0.0f (Autokit uses CropImageView.DEFAULT_ASPECT_RATIO).

Both Autokit and carlink_native show adapter SndPushInput non-zero totals during call.

claims.quote: | Adapter Sco send | Active during call | Active during call |

claims.statement: Working end-to-end mic data pipeline: Mac mic → Emulator AudioRecord (8kHz/16kHz) → carlink_native ring buffer → 20ms chunks (320B@8kHz / 640B@16kHz) → USB AudioData(0x07) [decodeType=3/5, volume=0.0, audioType=3] → Adapter MicAudioProcessor → AudioConvertor → SndPushInput (ALSA PCM bridge) → hfpd → BT SCO → Pixel → Google Meet.
claims.quote:
Mac mic → Emulator AudioRecord (8kHz/16kHz) → carlink_native ring buffer
  → 20ms chunks (320B@8kHz / 640B@16kHz)
  → USB AudioData(0x07) [decodeType=3/5, volume=0.0, audioType=3]
  → Adapter MicAudioProcessor → AudioConvertor
  → SndPushInput (ALSA PCM bridge) → hfpd → BT SCO → Pixel → Google Meet

claims.statement: The CarPlay + iPhone Microphone Session Capture (2026-03-15) is VERIFIED via live instrumented capture.
CarPlay capture environment was carlink_native on AAOS emulator + CPC200-CCPA (FW 2025.10.15.1127CAY) + iPhone via CarPlay (WiFi).
claims.quote: **Environment:** carlink_native on AAOS emulator + CPC200-CCPA (FW 2025.10.15.1127CAY) + iPhone via CarPlay (WiFi)

claims.statement: CarPlay test: Siri activation → Google Meet VOIP call → caller confirmed hearing mic audio.
claims.quote: **Test:** Siri activation → Google Meet VOIP call → caller confirmed hearing mic audio

claims.statement: CarPlay phone call audio path uses iAP2/WiFi (no BT HFP/SCO); Android Auto uses BT HFP/SCO via adapter.
claims.quote: | Phone call audio path | iAP2/WiFi (no BT HFP/SCO) | BT HFP/SCO via adapter |

claims.statement: CarPlay call mic decodeType is 5 (16kHz wideband); Android Auto is 3 (8kHz narrowband).
CarPlay call audio rate is 16000 Hz; Android Auto is 8000 Hz.
CarPlay mic negotiation: iPhone negotiates wideband; Android Auto: adapter forces narrowband for SCO.
claims.quote: | Mic negotiation | iPhone negotiates wideband | Adapter forces narrowband for SCO |

claims.statement: CarPlay does not use SCO bridge (audio over iAP2/WiFi); Android Auto uses SCO bridge (Sco send/read active).
claims.quote: | SCO bridge used | **No** — audio over iAP2/WiFi | **Yes** — `Sco send/read` active |

claims.statement: SndPushInput rate is 16000 samples/sec for both CarPlay and Android Auto (ALSA side).

CarPlay routes telephony audio through the iAP2 WiFi session (same path as Siri), so the adapter never needs to bridge to BT SCO — which is why CarPlay calls always worked with decodeType=5 hardcoded.
claims.quote: This is why CarPlay calls always worked with decodeType=5 hardcoded — the iPhone negotiates 16kHz wideband for all voice, including phone calls.

claims.statement: CarPlay Siri session (ttyLog) bitmask transitions: 0x0004 (Siri output) → 0x0404 (INPUT_CONFIG + Siri) → 0x0504 (recording + Siri) → 0x0000 (idle), with StartRecordMic(1) and StopRecordMic(2) commands, duration ~9s, identical bitmask pattern to AA Google Assistant.
claims.quote: Duration: ~9s. Identical bitmask pattern to AA Google Assistant.

claims.statement: _SendPhoneCommandToCar: StartRecordMic(1) starts recording; StopRecordMic(2) stops recording.

CarPlay VOIP Call #1 (ttyLog) bitmask transitions: 0x0001 (phone call mode) → 0x0401 (INPUT_CONFIG + call) → 0x0501 (recording + call) → 0x0000 (idle), with INPUT_CONFIG decodeType=5 16kHz, duration ~66s.
CarPlay VOIP calls use the same 0x0501 bitmask as AA VOIP calls — the adapter uses the same internal state for both protocols.
claims.quote: Note: **same `0x0501` bitmask as AA VOIP calls** — the adapter uses the same internal state for both protocols.

claims.statement: CarPlay VOIP Call #2 (ttyLog) showed the same pattern: 0x0001, INPUT_CONFIG, StartRecordMic(1), 0x0401 → 0x0501, still active at capture time.
claims.quote: Same pattern, second call. Still active at capture time.

claims.statement: During CarPlay VOIP the emulator AUDIO_PERF shows active:16000Hz 16000Hz:50ms(ACTIVE) Focus:[PHONE_CALL], confirming 16kHz playback (not 8kHz like AA).
During CarPlay VOIP the adapter SndPushInput increases by 16000/sec (16kHz rate), reaching total 3,645,440 samples = ~228 seconds of continuous mic audio.
claims.quote: SndPushInput total 3645440                 ← ~228 seconds of mic audio captured

claims.statement: Corrected 2026-05-21: audioInputFormats is NOT an iAP2 field; it belongs to the AirPlay WifiAudioFormats array sent over RTSP:5000 during AirPlay session SETUP (after the BT→WiFi handover).
claims.quote: AirPlay **`WifiAudioFormats`** array sent over **RTSP:5000** during **AirPlay session SETUP**

claims.statement: iAP2 0x1D01 IdentificationInformation carries transports / vehicle identity / message IDs only — no audio format.

From the AppleCarPlay AirPlay SETUP exchange: audioInputFormats = 16 (0x10, PCM 16-bit 16 kHz mono, mic/telephony input), audioInputFormats = 67108864 (0x4000000, AAC-ELD 48 kHz stereo duplex), supportsRTPPacketRedundancy = true.
claims.quote:
audioInputFormats : 16          ← 0x10  PCM 16-bit 16 kHz mono (mic / telephony input)
audioInputFormats : 67108864    ← 0x4000000  AAC-ELD 48 kHz stereo duplex
supportsRTPPacketRedundancy : true

claims.statement: audioInputFormats values are the AirPlay format bitmask: 16 = 0x10 (PCM 16 kHz mono), 67108864 = 0x4000000 (AAC-ELD 48 kHz duplex); advertised to iPhone inside the WifiAudioFormats array during AirPlay SETUP, from which the iPhone selects a rate for Siri and telephony.
claims.quote:
`audioInputFormats` values are the **AirPlay format bitmask** — `16` = `0x10` (PCM 16 kHz mono),
`67108864` = `0x4000000` (AAC-ELD 48 kHz duplex).

claims.statement: supportsRTPPacketRedundancy = true is a real AirPlay-layer SETUP field (RTP redundancy negotiation), not an iAP2 field.
claims.quote:
`supportsRTPPacketRedundancy : true` is likewise a real **AirPlay-layer** SETUP
field (RTP redundancy negotiation), not an iAP2 field.

claims.statement: The host→phone microphone uplink path is advertised via the audioInputFormats field on the telephony / speechRecognition / default rows of the AirPlay WifiAudioFormats array (0x4000000 = AAC-ELD duplex), plus compatibility/100 audioInputFormats = 0x10 (PCM 16 kHz mono).
Unlike AA, CarPlay VOIP calls show no Sco send or Sco read entries.
claims.quote: Unlike AA, CarPlay VOIP calls show **no** `Sco send` or `Sco read` entries.

claims.statement: SCO link activity observed during testing was from the earlier AA session: __DisconnectSco 1 1 0 -1 (SCO disconnected previous AA session), SCO MTU 240:32 Voice 0x0060 (re-negotiated), AG DE:AD:BE:EF:00:06 Disconnected (iPhone BT HFP disconnected).
claims.quote:
07:52:28  __DisconnectSco 1 1 0 -1!!!     ← SCO disconnected (previous AA session)
07:52:39  SCO MTU: 240:32 Voice: 0x0060   ← SCO re-negotiated for new BT connection
07:54:03  AG DE:AD:BE:EF:00:06: Disconnected  ← iPhone BT HFP disconnected

claims.statement: CarPlay telephony is fully WiFi-based via iAP2 protocol — the adapter's hfpd/SCO bridge is not involved.
claims.quote: CarPlay telephony is fully WiFi-based via iAP2 protocol — the adapter's `hfpd`/SCO bridge is not involved.

claims.statement: CarPlay vs AA init commands are the same: UseCarMic(7) + UseBoxTransAudio(23).
CarPlay vs AA Siri/GA start: AudioSignal_SIRI_START → 0x0504, same for both.
CarPlay vs AA Siri/GA mic both 16kHz, decodeType=5.
CarPlay vs AA call start: AudioSignal_PHONECALL_START → 0x0501, same bitmask for both.
CarPlay call INPUT_CONFIG is decodeType=5 (16kHz wideband); AA is decodeType=3 (8kHz narrowband).
CarPlay call mic capture is 16kHz mono 640B/20ms; AA is 8kHz mono 320B/20ms.
CarPlay call audio transport is iAP2/WiFi (DMSDP RTP); AA is BT SCO (hfpd bridge).
claims.quote: | **Call audio transport** | iAP2/WiFi (DMSDP RTP) | BT SCO (hfpd bridge) |

claims.statement: CarPlay adapter call bridge: MicAudioProcessor → iAP2 RTP; AA: MicAudioProcessor → ALSA → hfpd → SCO.
claims.quote: | **Adapter call bridge** | MicAudioProcessor → iAP2 RTP | MicAudioProcessor → ALSA → hfpd → SCO |

claims.statement: CarPlay SndPushInput rate is 16kHz (16000 samples/sec); AA is 16kHz on ALSA (internal).
CarPlay SCO active = No; AA SCO active = Yes (Sco send 240).
CarPlay total mic samples = 3,645,440 samples (228s at 16kHz); AA varies by call duration.
claims.quote: | **Total mic samples** | 3,645,440 samples (228s at 16kHz) | Varies by call duration |

claims.quote: - Live captures: 2026-03-15 Autokit + carlink_native VOIP sessions on AAOS emulator + CPC200-CCPA + Pixel 10 wireless AA
